channel.title: The Ansible Playbook — YouTube Comments
channel.description: New comments on The Ansible Playbook YouTube channel

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Ansible Automation Platform 2.6"
item.description: We actually just released AAP 2.7, so check it out! The workflow visualizer is completely redone.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Check out this video: https://youtu.be/qzSkA2M0wQc?si=Vdcm99LASg7uq79L

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Automate VM Lifecycle with OpenShift Virtualization & Ansible | Quick Demo"
item.description: All source code for Ansible can be found on github.com/ansible/ansible

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Five great use cases for Ansible Network Automation"
item.description: Check out this customer case from Southwest airlines that use Ansible Network Automation: https://www.redhat.com/en/resources/southwest-airlines-customer-story or ANZ bank: https://www.redhat.com/en/about/press-releases/anz-new-zealand-builds-collaboration-through-automation-red-hat

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Automate in concert"
item.description: Thank you for watching! We appreciate you.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform Overview"
item.description: Awesome to hear, thank you for watching!

item.title: @bobby07241 on "Build an Execution Environment with the Automation Portal"
item.description: This is amazing unfortunately the platform is unpayable , we cant afford it

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Build an Execution Environment with the Automation Portal"
item.description: I understand that budget can be a challenge, but I appreciate you tuning in to learn about the workflow! [1 likes]

item.title: @andrewm659 on "Build an Execution Environment with the Automation Portal"
item.description: Is this for the self-hosted version as well?

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Build an Execution Environment with the Automation Portal"
item.description: You can deploy this on Red Hat OpenShift or deploy a VM appliance: https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_ansible_automation_platform/2.7/install-con_self_service_rhel_appliances [1 likes]

item.title: @almircandidodepaula7171 on "Build an Execution Environment with the Automation Portal"
item.description: This is amazing! 👏👏👏 [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Build an Execution Environment with the Automation Portal"
item.description: We think so too!

item.title: Reply: @Hichamhe-shamMourad on "Infrastructure lifecycle management with Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform + HashiCorp Terraform"
item.description: Yes, for sure. Once the resources are created in AWS by HashiCorp Terraform, then Ansible Automation Platform (AAP) synchronizes that inventory details into the AAP inventory. Now that AAP is aware of those resources and their details, any ongoing automation that AAP needs to run against those resources can take place. [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "5 Use-cases with MCP server for Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: I appreciate it! -Sean

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "5 Use-cases with MCP server for Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: I love messing with Nuno -Sean

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "AAP 2.6: Introducing the Ansible Lightspeed intelligent assistant"
item.description: No, not anymore! You can use on-premises models in a solution with Red Hat AI. For example you could get Red Hat inference server which uses VLLM and do it entirely on-premises if you wanted.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Ansible Automation Platform 2.6"
item.description: We just released AAP 2.7, check it out! It is now just called automation portal.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Ansible Automation Platform 2.6"
item.description: With AAP 2.7 we now call it "Automation code assistant" for the integration with VScode, and "Automation intelligent assistant" for the chatroom built-in to the AAP webUI. Both of these can be used with on-premises models now!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Deploy vLLM on AWS in under 10 Minutes!"
item.description: Thank you for watching! I know Roger appreciates it, -Sean

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Patch Management with Ansible"
item.description: We have a lot of great source code on github.com/ansible/product-demos, let us know if you need some help navigating it!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Thank you! -Sean

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Step-by-Step: Installing Containerized AAP on RHEL9 | Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Sorry we missed this question, can you elaborate? This is containerized AAP on RHEL, it uses podman containers. There is also an OpenShift operator you can use.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Getting started with Event-Driven Ansible"
item.description: Correct, older video. I should add some info to the description that its older.. or pull the video entirely.

item.description: Can you write a demo code for this operation?

item.title: Reply: @piledriver_mtg on "AAP 2.6: Introducing the self-service automation portal"
item.description: It provides with a simplified version of job templates, and for non experienced Ansible users it does make difference. As for git, it's where you usually store your Ansible automations, and then attach it to a project so that AAP can pick up all Ansible playbooks from a particular repo.

item.title: @bulentsenguler3012 on "AAP 2.6: Introducing the self-service automation portal"
item.description: Hello, what does the purpose of gitlab or github integration? Actually, I do not understand why we need and How does this contribute? Because, AAP gives all job templates.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "AAP 2.6: Introducing the self-service automation portal"
item.description: We have a version for RHEL coming in November of 2025! We just got finished with the OpenShift version first! [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "AAP 2.6: Setting up RBAC with Config as Code"
item.description: This is a certified content collection: https://console.redhat.com/ansible/automation-hub/repo/published/ansible/platform/

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Automated Backups of network device configurations"
item.description: Well... that is a choice 😉

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Patch Management with Ansible"
item.description: Great question! On RHEL 8 you can fix that by installing python39-dnf from AppStream, then point Ansible to /usr/bin/python3.9. If you’re using execution environments in Automation Platform, those already include the right Python version.

item.title: Reply: @MalfuncEddie on "Patch Management with Ansible"
item.description: ​ @AnsibleAutomation Hi not able to check right now but pretty sure python39-dnf does not exist. Would you please be so kind to link the resources?

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Patch Management with Ansible"
item.description: Great point! You’re absolutely right that there’s a lot of coding and testing involved behind the scenes. The goal of the video was to simplify the key concepts, but implementation definitely takes time and iteration. Thanks for highlighting that!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "ServiceNow catalog item order fulfillment with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description:
I think we backed up some of the code here: https://github.com/ansible-tmm/instruqt-track-backups/blob/3ba410332b5efaba214965333596a2ad043e32ec/event-driven-netops-2-5/03-snow-event-troubleshooting-copy/assignment.md?plain=1#L2
You are simply just relaying data from a fact/facts to a snow incident number... alternatively it might be worth looking at our AI + Ansible workshop, this is really cool where we use AI to help enrich tickets - Sean

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: We value diversity in our speakers — their expertise matters most, and accents are part of that global mix. Still, we’ll work on making it easier to follow along with captions. Thanks for watching!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description:
Hey, I totally get where you’re coming from. A lot has changed in the last five years, and not all of it’s easy to navigate at first. The move to Collections and the broader ecosystem model was a big shift, but it’s actually opened the door for faster innovation and more community-driven content for different platforms and use cases.
The docs and tooling are still catching up to that pace. The new VS Code extension, Navigator, and Automation Platform UI are improving quickly, and feedback like yours really helps guide where the team focuses next.
If you haven’t already, check out the latest Ansible Automation Platform releases and the community hub at docs.ansible.com and galaxy.ansible.com . There’s a lot of exciting progress happening around AI-assisted authoring, Event-Driven Ansible, and simpler playbook workflows.
Appreciate you taking the time to share this. I hope we can earn you back as a fan! ❤️

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: orchestrate

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Appreciate that! We’ve got more deep dives coming soon — any topics you’d like to see next?

item.description: Thank you Juan!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Thank you Ajay!

item.description: Fair point — that typo slipped past me, and you’re absolutely right that details matter. Thanks for keeping me on my toes!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Effortless Playbook Generation using Ansible Lightspeed with IBM watsonx Code Assistant"
item.description: Thanks! We now also have the Ansible Lightspeed Intelligent Assistant which works directly in the Ansible Automation Platform's webUI [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Streamline VMware to OpenShift Virtualization Migration with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Thanks for watching! Glad you found it useful!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What is an Ansible Playbook?"
item.description: Are you using a developer license? You can talk to us on forum.ansible.com but if you are a customer you can open a support case! Here is a KB on how to open a support case: https://access.redhat.com/solutions/1242003

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: We love Nuno!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo! Application metrics and Event-Driven Ansible"
item.description: Some systems don't have the ability to do an API call, or the opposite problem is scale, you could have hundreds of API calls coming from hundreds of devices causing issues that way without anyway to throttle. EDA creates an easy way to read in events from an event bus and fire an event off... but you are correct... even a webhook can be an EDA plugin.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Advanced image builder: A practical guide to deploying RHEL with automation"
item.description:
Image Builder (osbuild-composer) is designed specifically for Linux-based images like Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL), CentOS Stream, and Fedora. It doesn’t currently support creating or customizing Microsoft Windows Server images.
If you need to automate Windows image creation, you’d typically use tools such as Packer, Windows System Image Manager (SIM), or Sysprep — and you can still orchestrate those with Ansible to handle provisioning and configuration.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Making the case for Ansible: A practical guide for unlocking value and overcoming obstacles"
item.description: Totally agree! Even small automations can save huge amounts of time over the long run. Thanks for watching! 🙌

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Terraforming with Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description:
The Self-Paced labs are here: http://red.ht/ansible-labs The article "Ansible vs. Terraform, clarified" is here: https://www.redhat.com/en/topics/automation/ansible-vs-terraform
The blogs: Providing Terraform with that Ansible Magic is here: https://www.ansible.com/blog/providing-terraform-with-that-ansible-magic
The Ansible versus Terraform Demystified is here: https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/ansible-vs.-terraform-demystified?intcmp=7015Y000003t7aWQAQ
The two other blogs are now out of date and have been removed. Is there any other links you need? Sorry for the delay here.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: Getting started with Event-Driven Ansible and Ansible Rulebooks"
item.description: Thanks for watching and for the constructive criticism!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: Getting started with Event-Driven Ansible and Ansible Rulebooks"
item.description: Thank you!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Simplification as Service - Defining self-service for the everyday developer"
item.description: The capability is now called Self-service automation portal, and released GA with Ansible Automation Platform 2.6 [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Ansible Tips and Tricks"
item.description: Yes, Ansible can absolutely automate OS deployments (PXE/iPXE, Kickstart/Preseed/Ignition) — but it typically orchestrates the provisioning stack rather than replacing it. Think of Ansible as the glue that configures DHCP/TFTP/HTTP, pushes boot/kickstart files, flips servers to PXE via out-of-band (Redfish/iDRAC/iLO), and then runs post-install config once SSH is up. [2 likes]

item.title: Reply: @anonymous9t1 on "Ansible Tips and Tricks"
item.description: ​ @AnsibleAutomation thank you so much for the detailed answers

item.description: Those are good use cases, however, the vmware_ops collection is not fully supported by Red Hat. It is just "validated" by Red Hat. I cannot understand why it is not fully supported...

item.title: @meovino on "Streamline VMware to OpenShift Virtualization Migration with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Thank you for creating this! Love to see how Ansible can further streamline this process. [1 likes]

item.title: @luisrocha6996 on "Simplification as Service - Defining self-service for the everyday developer"
item.description: I'm using AAP 2.4 in production and 2.5 in dev and I did not find any option to install service catalog. Could you please help?

item.title: @DanLinder on "ServiceNow ITSM + Ansible Automation"
item.description:
One of the neatest uses of Ansible + ServiceNow was at a Red Hat Summit ~3 years ago at a short talk given by UltaBeauty. In short, they first setup Ansible to capture the configurations from all their network devices and ensure their ServiceNow CMBD was current by updating the existing information (and opening a ticket if things had diverged). They later wrote playbooks to take the correct information (from the ServiceNow CMBD) and apply the settings to fix it (and opening a ticket to track the work). It then re-ran the configuration gather phase to validate the change was applied - if the change succeeded, then the ticket was closed as successful, but if the change failed then that too was noted and the ticket was escalated to have someone on the network team look into the issue). They iterated on the entire process over months and finally got to the point they were comfortable with the reporting and actions, they were able to free up their support staff to work on actual problems not spot-check the equipment.
And because this was fully automated, instead of doing the spot-checks and hoping they could touch their entire network once a quarter, they were able to have the system run DAILY and ensure any mis-configurations were found and corrected right away.
This was at the same Summit that Event Driven Ansible (a component of Ansible Automation Platform) was announced. I could imagine they have progressed to the point where the remote devices check-in each time a configuration change is made and Ansible is able to validate and correct the configuration in seconds rather than waiting up to a day later.

item.title: @frankwu5824 on "ServiceNow ITSM + Ansible Automation"
item.description: #ServiceNow #ansible [1 likes]

item.title: @ColinMcNaughton on "Automated Backups of network device configurations"

item.title: @tehwigzen on "Automated Backups of network device configurations"

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Automated Backups of network device configurations"
item.description: It might just be nice to snapshot configs before you make changes so its easier to roll back... but you are right, as you move towards IaC (infrastructure as code) your running configs become increasingly less important. Good feedback! -Sean [1 likes]

item.title: @kamilmonticolo on "Automated Backups of network device configurations"
item.description: Actually you did say nothing interesting

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Automated Backups of network device configurations"
item.description: You can checkout some playbook & role examples here: https://github.com/network-automation/toolkit, sorry you didn't like the video -Sean [2 likes]

item.title: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: Model Context Protocol (MCP) + Ansible Lightspeed in Future Automation Workflows"
item.description:
🔔 IMPORTANT DISCLAIMER – PLEASE READ
This is a conceptual demo from AnsibleFest 2025, intended to showcase possible future capabilities of Ansible Automation Platform with AI assistance and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
🚫 This functionality is not currently in the product, and what you see here does not represent a confirmed roadmap. 🧠 This is not RPA — the UI changes you see are visual guides to help illustrate what MCP could enable in the future. ✅ Policy enforcement with OPA is a real current feature in Ansible Automation Platform today.
This demo is designed to inspire and highlight how AI and automation might work together to simplify complex IT operations in the future.
Thanks for watching — we’d love to hear your thoughts!
#Ansible #AI #Automation #Disclaimer #ConceptDemo [2 likes]

item.title: @MalfuncEddie on "Patch Management with Ansible"
item.description: How would you resolve the issue that there are no python bindings for dnf for python 3.9 in RHEL 8 and ansible 2.17 and above require python 3.9. it's either stays with 2.16 or you can't patch rhel 8

item.title: @frankwu5824 on "ServiceNow catalog item order fulfillment with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Excellent [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "ServiceNow catalog item order fulfillment with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Thank you! Cheers!

item.title: @sdunneyt on "ServiceNow catalog item order fulfillment with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Terrific demo! [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "ServiceNow catalog item order fulfillment with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Thanks!

item.title: @njappboy on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: I stepped away from Ansible for 5 years and came back to the tool to find the current state a mess. Documentation exists but it is poor. VSCode Extension is lacking and incomplete, many functions are in "coming soon" state. The new collections and roles grouping making versioning a monolithic step backwards. Completely disappointed but expected from RHEL at this point, which I really wish wasn't the case. [1 likes]

item.title: @ahmedal-hujazi8497 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Even the voice recognition software indicates he says "...help our customers or castrate their automation content..." Castrate???? What did Anshul actually say????

item.title: @AlaVRSim on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: would be great if aap can be the center of automation and the AIO tool with a git built-in.

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Hey Sean here from REd Hat, have you checked out the CaC (configuration as code). There are some modules / content collections for managing AAP itself with git (infrastructure as code), some of our awesome consultants put together this workshop if you want to try it out! https://aap2.demoredhat.com/exercises/instruqt/aapcasc-4

item.title: @shinjithkv9151 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Very informative session [1 likes]

item.title: @shokrymohamed2285 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Can we upgrade from 2.4 or still upgrade not available?

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Hey Sean here from Red Hat, you can now upgrade to AAP 2.4 to AAP 2.5, for RPM -> RPM or container -> container, or Openshift->Openshift, but you can't do one type of install to another, I would encourage using a CaC method for that.

item.title: Reply: @shokrymohamed2285 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: @ thanks , i already do the upgrade 2 month ago

item.title: @xP0STx on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Is an automation gateway node and event driven node now required for all 2.5 installs? I am comparing inv files between both, and my 2.4 only has controller, execution, and hub nodes. Looks really awesome though, I am excited!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: We're glad you're excited - and good catch. The automation gateway node and event-driven node will be required for 2.5 installs in order to get access to the unified UI features highlighted in the video. Information regarding the upgrade path from 2.4 will be published in the coming month. Stay tuned!

item.title: @JuanMedina-h1q on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Excellent overview [2 likes]

item.title: @ajaychenampara8012 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Fantastic summary! [1 likes]

item.title: @KirbyHung on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: brilliant [1 likes]

item.title: @dipadityadas on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Awesome [2 likes]

item.title: @livn.686 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: A bit unintentionally funny at times but still informative. When will the migration procedure from v. 2.4 be available ? [4 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: targeting the next release, before mid-November, we have a timer on the wall :)

item.title: Reply: @zarghamkhan856 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: @AnsibleAutomation Any update on the migration procedure to v2.5? Can't wait! :)

item.title: @dylangaliffa7142 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: The hand waves are great! 😀 [8 likes]

item.title: @JakubVeverka-v2z on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Hi, ad infra.hub_configuration . Is it a typo, or new version of ah_configuration? btw hub_configuration would be a much better name for it... [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Yes it is changing names, check out this forum post: https://forum.ansible.com/t/promoting-infra-ah-configuration-to-certified-collection-ansible-ah/8276 [2 likes]

item.title: Reply: @JakubVeverka-v2z on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description:
@AnsibleAutomation well, the collection will have its downstream name ansible.ah, but the infra collection is not being renamed. At least not according to the post you've shared. I quote
Roles and other playbooks from the infra.ah_configuration collection will remain community-supported and stay in infra.ah_configuration collection. [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: @JakubVeverka-v2z You are right, it was super confusing! Just had Emily Bock (Product Manager for Automation hub) add some clarity to the forum post. Try refreshing now. [1 likes]

item.title: @frankwu5824 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: Best technical marketing team in the biz [9 likes]

item.title: Reply: @seancavanaugh3817 on "What's new: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform 2.5"
item.description: ❤ [1 likes]

item.title: @sebastiandunne9418 on "Dynamically Add CPU and Memory to VMware VMs with Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Brilliant - we're getting closer and closer to the "software-defined datacenter". [3 likes]

item.title: @amitashtoorkar543 on "Automate VMware VM Provisioning in Minutes with Ansible"
item.description: What connection method do you use for this automation? I am struggling to connect with vcenter by providing vcenter_host, vcenter_user, vcenter_password. Please share the github link for code

item.title: @Nonuabizbish on "Automate VMware VM Provisioning in Minutes with Ansible"
item.description: plz just show the URL...

item.title: @chuckjamm on "Automate VMware VM Provisioning in Minutes with Ansible"
item.description: Does the playbook have sections that maps port groups to network interfaces?

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Automate VMware VM Provisioning in Minutes with Ansible"
item.description: It does. An example of what that would look like can be found here from my repo: https://github.com/rlopez133/ocp-virt/blob/main/collections/ansible_collections/rlopez/ocp_virt/roles/virt_plan/tasks/10_create_virt_plan.yml#L47

item.title: @Kkgagno1 on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Is this gui usable in an all Ubuntu environment?

item.title: Reply: @RedHatDavidDanielsson on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: There are some cloud offerings that would work in an Ubuntu only environment, but if you were looking to install it on-prem AAP requires RHEL (it does come with some RHEL subs)

item.title: @johnhardman7986 on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Any chance you can share the code from the demo? [3 likes]

item.title: @alphonsesuitperson on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: This is the way, David knows his stuff, if you aren't doing it this way, you should ask WHY?? [2 likes]

item.title: Reply: @aaronrobinson2121 on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description:
I'm a little confused; this role (now infra.aap_configuration but the same thing) reduces everything down to variable yml files? Would I want that? I've been setting up my stuff using ansible.controller directly so far, and I appreciate the discoverability in VSCode where the dot notation lets you know what you can do, or the "Go to Definition" just brings up the code which has examples in it of how to use them. You lose that when you use this role, or am I missing something?
Also I'm struggling to find any courses in the learning center, or any documentation outlining this way of doing things, outside of this one video.

item.title: @Bluealein56 on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: This is the way!!!!!! David implemented this framework for my orgs instances of AAP and it has saved us hundreds of hours and makes managing AAP easier. [4 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: Configuration as Code for Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: We agree David is awesome -Sean

item.title: @bytemack695 on "Management vs Automation"
item.description: Am from Zambia I have your shirt, and I want a Job

item.title: @TertiusBouwer-r9r on "Management vs Automation"
item.description: Love your work Legend

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Management vs Automation"
item.description: We love Tertius - Sean

item.title: @studiogerk on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: Sorry you lost all credibility with that horrid text on the screen with mispellings. If you can't spell (or be bothered to fix the auto generated text of) ROLL then your advice is honestly quite suspect. This is either laziness, ignorance or bad spelling/grammar. Not trying to fry you, just giving advice on how not to lose the next person looking for advice that stumbles in and goes WTF?!? CAN'T EVEN SPELL ROLE. NEXT. [1 likes]

item.title: @talassiofilo7471 on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: Hi :) Which editor do you use? Visual Code or other? thanks :)

item.title: @brunorocha2787 on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: Ansible Rolls!!! [5 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: The best new sushi rolls -Sean

item.title: @hannes2429 on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: Why you don’t use FQCNs? Since Ansible Core 2.16 they’re mandatory [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: The video was originally in 9:16 format (vertical) for YouTube #shorts so I recorded everything in 9:16 and it was hard to make the text readable with the FQCN because of the width. It is best practice to use FQCN and it's good to call me out and shame me! The content collections "should" have a meta/runtime.yml file that will convert the non-FQCN to FQCN to run, but you are correct that we should always use FQCN to avoid content collection collisions - Sean Cavanaugh [1 likes]

item.title: @MaplePengiun on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: A few times in the video you use the term "playbook(s)" instead of "task file(s)". I'm aware this will come across as pedantic and in a pure workflow type setting I would agree that level of specificity may not be necessary. However, for educational/instructional videos I believe it is important to use correct terminology to avoid confusing people who are new to the concepts or terms being referenced. [5 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: I will do a break down of a role vs playbook and why I combined them here. The goal is writing better playbooks which I think should include roles and maybe should have been more clear on the difference? I think the video is quite clear but my title and description might not be? - Sean

item.title: @marcbaudoin3081 on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: Don't trust articifial intelligence to write your (large) subtitles. This is 100 % artificial and 0 % intelligence. It should have written "roles", not "rolls"... [4 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Write better Ansible Playbooks!"
item.description: That was my bad... the transcribing software is pretty good, but I have to manually fix some words and I somehow missed one. -Sean [1 likes]

item.title: @adamasimolowo8285 on "Effortless Playbook Generation using Ansible Lightspeed with IBM watsonx Code Assistant"
item.description: Nice gen AI use case for ansible. [1 likes]

item.title: @jgrote on "Effortless Playbook Generation using Ansible Lightspeed with IBM watsonx Code Assistant"
item.description: "We dont need to add things like write me a playbook" The prompt: "I want to write a playbook that will..." 🤔

item.title: @frankwu5824 on "Effortless Playbook Generation using Ansible Lightspeed with IBM watsonx Code Assistant"
item.description: 💪🏽 [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Effortless Playbook Generation using Ansible Lightspeed with IBM watsonx Code Assistant"
@angeln8967 on "Demo: All together now! Datadog, AWS SQS and Ansible"
item.description: Where I can found the rulebooks for consume ansible.eda.aws_sqs_queue:

item.title: @victorcasado534 on "Demo: All together now! Datadog, AWS SQS and Ansible"
item.description: Thanks for the demo. I'm just updating the AAP to 2.4 to interface EDA with DATADOG in my organization. Do you know if an official certified DD collection will be released soon at the HUB? I only see that the collection has a module to install the agents. Thank you!! [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: All together now! Datadog, AWS SQS and Ansible"
item.description: There are ongoing discussions around the expansion of the existing Datadog collection

item.title: @SeanCavanaugh1986 on "Demo: All together now! Datadog, AWS SQS and Ansible"
item.description: Really cool demo. Can this also work with Microsoft Azure Cloud? [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Demo: All together now! Datadog, AWS SQS and Ansible"
item.description: Hey Sean! A great stand in for SQS in this demo would be Azure Service Bus. Ansible supports this integration! AND guess what, my guy?! Datadog also has support for Azure Service Bus actions through their Workflow tool! It should be quite straightforward to recreate this in Azure and that plays right into the multi-cloud automation capabilities of THE Ansible Automation Platform. Thanks for the question, friend!

item.title: @ahmedhamouda-wo6ol on "Automate VM Lifecycle with OpenShift Virtualization & Ansible | Quick Demo"
item.description: Red Hat Ansible Automation , Could you please share the source code for Ansible? Thank you! [3 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Network Compliance Dashboard - "Dynamic Documentation""
item.description: Tony is awesome [1 likes]

item.title: @sgtkeebler on "What is an Ansible Playbook?"
item.description: I am trying to learn Ansible. I had zero problems installing Ansible tower on my corporate infrastructure, and I am trying to install ansible automation platform for days now and just been hitting error after error. After trying to install Automation platform and rebooting just today, I can no longer log into my rhel system anymore. My inventory file is good as well. I am just at the point of giving up.

item.title: Reply: @BOOSTEDDUDE on "What is an Ansible Playbook?"
item.description: Did you install on a VM? If so, do you have a snapshot you can revert to?

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "What is an Ansible Playbook?"
item.description: Our pleasure! Thanks for watching!

item.title: @Twst704 on "Event Driven Ansible for Networks "Don't Shut my ports!!!""
item.description: Tony, this was great! However ... is there a "Setting this all up from scratch" video? I understand the concepts at a high level (it all made sense), but could really use some assistance setting up each of the individual components shown in this ecosystem. Thanks in advance!

item.title: Reply: @Ansible4Networking on "Event Driven Ansible for Networks "Don't Shut my ports!!!""
item.description: Surething, I'll circle back with some links and steps to show how to do this example from the ground up. Most likely by tomorrow. [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @Twst704 on "Event Driven Ansible for Networks "Don't Shut my ports!!!""
item.description: @Ansible4Networking Can’t wait!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Event Driven Ansible for Networks "Don't Shut my ports!!!""
item.description: Please see this Gilab/README file for details on how EDA and AAP were set up for the demo. https://gitlab.com/redhatautomation/no_shut_eda_cisco.git - Tony

item.title: @shariarekhan3593 on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: can anyone please share that URL at 0:31 please

item.title: @camkerr81 on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Great tutorials BTW. [2 likes]

item.title: @camkerr81 on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Did you have an updated tutorial for Ansible AP 2.5. I'm fairly new to this and from what I've found it's alot different to 2.4. Like no ability to run adjoc jobs on a host etc [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description:
ad-hoc (run command) is still there for the hosts in an inventory. There are some UI changes to get to the inventory ("infrastructure" > "inventory", instead of "resources" > inventory"), but other than that, it's the same.
You can find more in the documentation: https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_ansible_automation_platform/2.5/html-single/using_automation_execution/index#proc-cont[…]c-commands [1 likes]

item.title: @SPX-0-day on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: Hi thanks for the video, how to setup/download windows_EE? is there any community image available to pull ? because the default image "ee-supported-rhel8:latest" not working. [1 likes]

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "Connecting Windows server to Ansible Automation Platform"
item.description: You're very welcome!

item.title: Reply: @AnsibleAutomation on "ansible-lint walkthrough"
item.description: Forgiven [1 likes]
